title: Vehicle technicians, mechanics and electricians
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 27 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Dismount, mount, and repair or replace tires, including locating punctures and sealing them.
Connect test panels to engines and measure performance indicators like ignition timing and generator output.
Install, repair, or replace safety glass and related components, including weatherproofing windows with rubber strips.
Identify and repair motorcycle subassemblies, including forks, transmissions, and brakes, and conduct tests for quality.
Dismantle, repair, or replace defective parts such as engines, carburetors, and generators using hand and power tools.
Inspect and maintain automotive equipment, including brakes, steering mechanisms, and wheel alignment, to ensure proper functioning.
Conduct routine vehicle maintenance such as oil changes, lubrications, and tune-ups, and perform repairs on various systems.
Test drive vehicles to diagnose malfunctions and ensure proper operation of components and systems.
Reassemble and align engines and subassemblies after repairs, ensuring correct installation and functionality of parts.
Document inspection findings, repairs performed, and recommend future work required on vehicles to customers.
Conduct visual inspections and tests to diagnose vehicle problems, utilizing various diagnostic tools and instruments.
Clean and maintain work areas to ensure efficiency and safety in automotive repair operations.
Use handtools and power tools, such as screwdrivers, wrenches, pneumatic wrenches, and lathes, for vehicle repairs and installations.
Diagnose and repair vehicle heating and cooling systems, including testing and adjusting exhaust emissions devices.
Inspect repaired vehicles for proper functioning, dimensional accuracy, and overall appearance, and test-drive to ensure alignment and handling.
Disassemble appliances for diagnosis and repairs, and reassemble after maintenance, ensuring all components are clean and lubricated.
Record maintenance information, including repairs performed, parts used, and issues identified during inspections.
Install, maintain, or repair security systems, alarm devices, and related equipment according to blueprints and electrical plans.
Inspect and test various equipment, including electrical connections and circuits, to locate damage or worn parts and diagnose malfunctions.
Follow supervisors' instructions on parts restoration or replacement, and assess the time required for each repair job.
Record meter readings, installation data, and maintenance logs, and prepare documents such as invoices or warranties.
Maintain and organize stocks of parts for on-site installation, maintenance, and repair of vehicles and appliances.
Test and repair domestic electrical or gas appliances, following wiring and system specifications.
Remove or disassemble defective automatic mechanical door closers, and repair or replace worn or broken door parts using hand tools.
Set doors into place, stack hardware sections, and carry springs to install spring systems after rail or track installation.
Inspect job sites to determine appropriateness of door for a given location and check safety regulations and building codes.
Adjust doors, safety controls, counterweights, and components such as valves and seals; and operate lifts, winches, or chain falls.
Connect electrical wiring to control panels and electric motors, and install electrical equipment such as air-conditioning and ignition systems.
Study blueprints and schematic diagrams to determine appropriate methods of installing or repairing automated door openers and fixtures.
Perform routine maintenance such as cleaning, oiling parts, and testing equipment for overheating and proper operation.
Fabricate replacements for worn parts using welders and shaping or milling machines, and assemble, install, repair, and maintain various equipment.
Check that equipment and systems meet specifications and compliance standards, and maintain inventories of spare parts for all equipment.
Diagnose problems and repair or replace defective parts such as motors, wiring, and components, using diagnostic instruments and hand tools.
Inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to locate and diagnose malfunctions using visual inspections and testing devices.
Obtain problem descriptions from customers and prepare cost estimates for repairs based on necessary parts and labor.
Run tests on machinery and equipment for compliance with standards, and prepare records detailing tests, repairs, and maintenance.
Analyze test data to diagnose malfunctions and evaluate effects of modifications on systems' performance characteristics.
Advise management on product performance, customer satisfaction, and suggestions for product improvements, alongside providing technical customer service.
Inspect and test components for accurate assembly, defects, and to ensure proper installation of various systems and machinery.
Reassemble engines after repair or maintenance work, and ensure proper functioning through testing and adjustments.
Coordinate efforts with other workers in installing or maintaining equipment, and consult with customers to plan layouts or resolve issues.
Analyze project requests and proposals to determine feasibility and cost of aerospace or aeronautical products.
Write and prepare technical reports, documentation, or proposals for use by engineering staff and management.
Plan or conduct experimental tests on models or prototypes of aircraft or aerospace systems or equipment.
Research customer design proposals to evaluate feasibility, cost, and maintenance requirements of designs.
Coordinate activities of technical personnel involved in designing, modifying, or testing aerospace products.
Confer with engineers to resolve system malfunctions and implement operating procedures.
Conduct analytical studies to develop designs for products like marine engines and structures.
Design and supervise testing and repair of marine equipment and systems, including system integration.
Evaluate power output and environmental impact of fuel cell systems and alternative power sources.
Conduct research in automotive engineering to develop new technologies and improve fuel efficiency.
Monitor tool and part inventories, ensuring adequate maintenance and safe working conditions in shops.
Inspect and analyze systems to determine needed installations or repairs, ensuring conformance to standards.
Conduct worker training in safe repair or maintenance techniques and operational procedures.
Compute estimates and actual costs for materials, labor, and other project factors.
Discuss engineering plans with clients to evaluate their needs and make necessary changes.

emerging_tasks.task: Inspect and install lights, such as headlights and taillights.
Repair or replace engine mechanical equipment, such as power tilts, water pumps, bilge pumps, or power take-offs.
Use drones for inspection of high-voltage lines and other hard-to-reach equipment.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
